q: What does the German word "hart" mean?
e: Hart (adjective) means "hard". Example: Das Leben ist hart. (Life is hard.)

q: What does "unbedingt" mean in English?
e: Unbedingt (adjective) means "absolute" or "unconditional". Example: Ich muss den Zug unbedingt kriegen. (I absolutely must catch the train.)

q: What does "das Alter" mean?
e: Das Alter (noun) means "age". Example: Sie starb im Alter von 75 Jahren. (She died at the age of 75 years.)

q: What does "der Kilometer" mean?
e: Der Kilometer (noun) means "kilometre". Example: Von Leipzig nach München sind es etwa 430 Kilometer. (From Leipzig to Munich is about 430 kilometres.)

q: What does "grundsätzlich" mean?
e: Grundsätzlich (adjective) means "fundamental" or "in principle". Example: Ich bin grundsätzlich anderer Meinung. (In principle, I have a different opinion.)

q: What does the verb "erhöhen" mean?
d: Auf jeden Fall, in jedem Fall
e: Erhöhen (verb) means "to increase" or "to raise". Example: Wir müssen unser Angebot erhöhen. (We must increase our offer.)

q: What does "der Vertrag" mean?
e: Der Vertrag (noun) means "contract". Example: Der Vertrag gilt ab 1. Januar 2006. (The contract is valid from 1st January 2006.)

q: What does "die Hilfe" mean?
e: Die Hilfe (noun) means "help" or "assistance". Example: Wir hoffen auf eure Hilfe. (We are counting on your help.)

s: The word "dollar" means: a unit of currency used in various countries
q: Which English word means 'a unit of currency used in various countries'?
e: Der Dollar (noun) means "dollar". Example: Der Dollar steht gut im Kurs. (The dollar is trading well.)

q: What does "das Verfahren" mean?
e: Das Verfahren (noun) means "method", "process", or "technique". Example: Dieses Verfahren ist am effektivsten. (This method is the most effective.)

q: What does the adverb "zuerst" mean?
e: Zuerst (adverb) means "first" or "at first". Example: Zuerst studiere ich, dann mache ich ein Praktikum. (First I study, then I do an internship.)

s: The word "theater" means: a building where plays, films, or performances are shown
q: Which English word means 'a building where plays, films, or performances are shown'?

q: What does "die Gefahr" mean?
e: Die Gefahr (noun) means "danger". Example: Er war in großer Gefahr. (He was in great danger.)

s: The word "patient" means: a person receiving medical treatment or care

e: Der Patient (noun) means "patient". Example: Der Arzt untersucht einen Patienten. (The doctor examines a patient.)

q: What does "die Kosten" mean?
e: Die Kosten (noun, plural) means "costs" or "expenses". Example: Die Kosten für die Reise übernimmt die Firma. (The company covers the travel costs.)

q: What does the verb "melden" mean?
e: Melden (verb) means "to report" or "to register". Example: Der Wetterbericht meldet Schnee für das Wochenende. (The weather report predicts snow for the weekend.)

q: What does "die Polizei" mean?
d: to jump up, to burst open
e: Die Polizei (noun) means "police". Example: Die Polizei erreicht den Unfallort zuerst. (The police arrive at the accident scene first.)

s: The word "computer" means: an electronic device that processes data and performs calculations

e: Der Computer (noun) means "computer". Example: Ich arbeite jeden Tag am Computer. (I work at the computer every day.)

q: What does the adjective "inner" mean?
e: Inner (adjective) means "internal" or "inner". Example: Das ist eine innere Angelegenheit. (This is an internal matter.)

q: What does "künftig" mean?
e: Künftig (adjective) means "future" or "coming". Example: Auch künftige Generationen müssen auf dieser Welt noch leben können. (Future generations must also be able to live on this world.)

q: What does the adverb "hinaus" mean?
e: Hinaus (adverb) means "out" or "beyond". Example: Darüber hinaus gibt es nichts zu berichten. (Beyond that, there is nothing to report.)

q: What does "das Gerät" mean?
e: Das Gerät (noun) means "tool", "piece of equipment", or "appliance". Example: Ein Thermometer ist ein Gerät zur Temperaturmessung. (A thermometer is a tool for measuring temperature.)

q: What does "vorhanden" mean?
e: Vorhanden (adjective) means "available" or "existing". Example: Es ist kein Papier mehr vorhanden. (There is no paper left available.)

q: What does "der Oktober" mean?
e: Der Oktober (noun) means "October". Example: Im Oktober ist Erntedankfest. (Thanksgiving is in October.)

q: What does "die Reihe" mean?
e: Die Reihe (noun) means "row" or "line". Example: Stellt euch bitte in einer Reihe auf! (Please line up in a row!)

q: What does "historisch" mean?
e: Historisch (adjective) means "historical". Example: In jeder Generation kommt es zu historischen Ereignissen. (Every generation experiences historical events.)

q: What does "der Stoff" mean?
e: Der Stoff (noun) means "material" or "substance". Example: Samt ist ein sehr weicher Stoff. (Velvet is a very soft material.)

q: What does "die Mitte" mean?
e: Die Mitte (noun) means "middle" or "center". Example: Kassel liegt in der Mitte Deutschlands. (Kassel is located in the middle of Germany.)

d: am besten oder Superlativ von gut
e: Lieber (adverb) means "rather" or "prefer". Example: Ich esse lieber Birnen als Äpfel. (I prefer to eat pears rather than apples.)

q: What does the verb "vorkommen" mean?
e: Vorkommen (verb) means "to happen", "to occur", or "to seem". Example: Fälle von Korruption kommen auch im Bundestag vor. (Cases of corruption also occur in the Bundestag.)

q: What does "aha" mean?
e: Aha (particle) is an exclamation meaning "aha" or "I see". Example: Aha, so ist das also! (Aha, so that's how it is!)

q: What does the verb "betonen" mean?
e: Betonen (verb) means "to stress" or "to emphasize". Example: Der Professor betont noch einmal, dass das Seminar Pflicht ist. (The professor emphasizes again that the seminar is mandatory.)

q: What does "das Glück" mean?
e: Das Glück (noun) means "luck" or "fortune". Example: Alles Gute und viel Glück zum Geburtstag! (All the best and good luck for your birthday!)

q: What does "dennoch" mean?
e: Dennoch (adverb) means "nevertheless" or "still". Example: Gregor hat sich große Mühe gegeben, dennoch hat er die Prüfung nicht bestanden. (Gregor made great effort, nevertheless he didn't pass the exam.)

q: What does "in der Mitte" mean?

q: What does "zuletzt" mean?

q: What does "die Ordnung" mean?

q: What does "der Osten" mean?

q: What is the German word for 'Sunday'?

q: What does 'meistens' mean?

q: What is the German word for 'examination' or 'investigation'?

e: Die Untersuchung is a feminine noun meaning 'examination' or 'investigation'. Example: Die Untersuchung findet am Montag statt. (The examination takes place on Monday.)

q: What does the German verb 'bestätigen' mean?
e: Bestätigen is a verb meaning 'to confirm' or 'to endorse'. Example: Das Gericht bestätigt seine Unschuld. (The court confirms his innocence.)

q: What is the German word for 'change'?
e: Die Veränderung is a feminine noun meaning 'change'. Example: Ich kann mich schwer auf Veränderungen einstellen. (I have difficulty adapting to changes.)

q: What does the German adverb 'überall' mean?
e: Überall is an adverb meaning 'everywhere'. Example: Überall freuen sich die Kinder auf die Ferien. (Children everywhere are looking forward to the holidays.)

q: What is the German word for 'data'?
e: Die Daten is a plural noun meaning 'data'. Example: Derzeit liegen uns keine Daten zu diesem Fall vor. (We currently have no data on this case.)

q: What does the German adverb 'andererseits' mean?
d: on the basis of, because of

q: What does the German adjective 'zahlreich' mean?

q: What does the German verb 'erlauben' mean?

e: Erlauben is a verb meaning 'to allow' or 'to permit'. Example: Die Eltern erlauben ihren Kindern, bis um neun draußen zu bleiben. (The parents allow their children to stay outside until nine.)

q: What does the German adjective 'aktiv' mean?
e: Aktiv is an adjective meaning 'active'. Example: Wir treiben in unserer Freizeit aktiv Sport. (We actively do sports in our leisure time.)

q: What is the German word for 'circle' or 'district'?

q: What is the German word for 'demand' or 'claim'?
d: Von etwas absehen oder sich beherrschen
e: Die Forderung is a feminine noun meaning 'demand' or 'claim'. Example: Der Chef kommt den Forderungen nach Gehaltserhöhung nicht nach. (The boss does not comply with the demands for a salary increase.)

q: What is the German word for 'speech' or 'talk'?
e: Die Rede is a feminine noun meaning 'speech' or 'talk'. Example: In dem Zeitungsartikel ist die Rede von Reformen. (The newspaper article talks about reforms.)

q: What does the German verb 'blicken' mean?
e: Blicken is a verb meaning 'to glance' or 'to look'. Example: Rainer blickt immer nach vorne. (Rainer always looks forward.)

q: What does the German adjective 'konkret' mean?
e: Konkret is an adjective meaning 'concrete'. Example: Nächste Woche ist keine sehr konkrete Zeitangabe. (Next week is not a very concrete time specification.)

q: What does the German adjective 'regelmäßig' mean?
e: Regelmäßig is an adjective meaning 'regular'. Example: Ich nehme regelmäßig an Fachtagungen teil. (I regularly participate in professional conferences.)

q: What does the German adjective 'ehemalig' mean?
e: Ehemalig is an adjective meaning 'former'. Example: Auf einem Klassentreffen treffen sich ehemalige Mitschüler. (At a class reunion, former classmates meet.)

q: What does the German adverb 'stets' mean?
e: Stets is an adverb meaning 'always'. Example: Das Kinoprogramm findet man stets aktuell im Internet. (You can always find the current movie program on the internet.)

q: What does the German verb 'versprechen' mean?
d: Zu welcher Zeit oder zu welchem Zeitpunkt
e: Versprechen is a verb meaning 'to promise'. Example: Sie verspricht ihm, nicht mehr zu rauchen. (She promises him not to smoke anymore.)
